subject: refactor(discover): extract metric pool + helpers to discoverData.js
body:
Audit finding extension to #16 — sections/Discover.jsx was 711 lines,
211 over the 500-line component soft-limit. Most of the mass came
from a 245-line array of 21 metric definitions (DISCOVER_METRICS),
the humorous file-name generator with two ~25-element word lists,
and the getRandomMetrics() picker — all pure data / pure functions
with zero React surface area.

Extracted to a new pure-data module:

  dashboard/js/sections/discover/discoverData.js  (335 lines)

The new file exports:
  - DISCOVER_METRICS — 21 metric definitions, unchanged from the
    original (label, description, calculate(commits))
  - getRandomMetrics(count, pinned) — initial-pick + shuffle picker
    that honours pinned slots
  - getHumorousFileName(path, cache) — adjective+noun generator with
    consumer-owned cache parameter

After:
  dashboard/js/sections/Discover.jsx: 711 → 430 lines (-281, well
    under the 500-line soft-limit)
  dashboard/js/sections/discover/discoverData.js: new (335 lines)

Discover.jsx now imports the three symbols from
`./discover/discoverData.js` and otherwise delegates exactly as
before — every state hook, effect, callback, and render branch is
unchanged. The two localStorage helpers (loadPinnedMetrics,
savePinnedMetrics) stayed in the React file because they're tightly
coupled to the component's pinned-state effect and have no other
consumer.

The new `discover/` subdirectory matches the `debug/` pattern from
the previous DebugPill split — section-specific helper modules live
in a same-named subfolder so they stay grouped with their consumer
without polluting the top-level sections/ listing.

Build clean (562.43 kB → 562.43 kB, identical post-tree-shake).
60/60 tests pass.

Tags: refactor, ui, code-organization, audit
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: vanilla-daisyui-cleanup
Semver: patch
